perturbation.original_text: 4. SUPPLIER shall maintain all Confidential Information received from GK in the same manner that the SUPPLIER maintains its own Confidential Information, provided that the standard of care required shall be at least a reasonable standard. Information disclosed in other that written form shall be considered Confidential Information only to the extent GK summarizes the same in a written form that is transmitted to SUPPLIER within thirty (30) calendar days of the non-written disclosures.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. SUPPLIER shall maintain all Confidential Information received from GK. Information disclosed in other that written form shall be considered Confidential Information only to the extent GK summarizes the same in a written form that is transmitted to SUPPLIER within thirty (30) calendar days of the non-written disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "in the same manner that the SUPPLIER maintains its own Confidential Information, provided that the standard of care required shall be at least a reasonable standard", the contract no longer specifies a minimum standard of care for protecting Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2, which mandates strict confidence but offers no metric for how that is measured or enforced. Now it is up to interpretation what it means to maintain all Confidential Information
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the supplier must maintain Confidential Information using at least a reasonable standard of care. The changed text removes this minimum standard, meaning there is no longer a defined level of care required. This directly contradicts Section 2, which demands 'strict confidence' but now lacks any metric for evaluating whether that standard is being met. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the supplier's obligations, making the 'strict confidence' requirement unenforceable. The removal makes Section 4 and Section 2 incompatible, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Confidential Information does not include the following: a) information which was already in the public domain at the time of disclosure; or b) information which, though originally confidential, subsequently becomes part of the public domain through no fault of SUPPLIER; or c) information which was properly in SUPPLIER’s possession prior to receipt thereof from GK, as reasonably evidenced by written records or other writings, or by actual use by SUPPLIER prior to the disclosure by GK. In each case, the (i) burden of pr oof shall lie with SUPPLIER to demonstrate that one of the above-listed exceptions applies and Sign:_____________ Page 2 (ii) SUPPLIER shall notify GKT in writing of SUPPLIER’s intent to make any disclosure based on one of the above-listed exceptions at least 30 days in advance (including a description of the informati on to be disclosed and the basis for the claimed exception).
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Confidential Information does not include information which was already publicly known. Sign:_____________ Page 2
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed exceptions to what constitutes Confidential Information, specifically the section that states that the burden of proof lies with the supplier, creates an internal contradiction. Section 2 states the supplier has to hold the information with "strict confidence". With the exception, it outlines who holds the burden of proof, but by not having that, there's no clear direction on how to handle the conflict between both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided exceptions to the definition of Confidential Information, including a burden of proof on the supplier and a notification requirement. The changed text removes these exceptions, making the definition of Confidential Information broader and stricter. This contradicts Section 2, which requires the supplier to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence. Without the exceptions, the supplier's obligations become less clear and potentially more onerous, as there are no longer defined limits or processes for disputing what constitutes Confidential Information. This creates a direct conflict between the strict confidentiality obligation in Section 2 and the undefined scope of Confidential Information after the modification, therefore it is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Any disclosure of Confidential Inform ation shall be limited SUPPLIER’s employees and who have a need to use or study such Confidential Information for the Purpose. SUPPLIER shall advise its employees and agen ts of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement, but SUPPLIER shall remain responsible to ensure their compliance.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Any disclosure of Confidential Information shall be limited SUPPLIER’s employees and who have a need to use or study such Confidential Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence that states "SUPPLIER shall advise its employees and agents of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement, but SUPPLIER shall remain responsible to ensure their compliance." now the SUPPLIER's agents and employees, are no longer binded by anything which creates an in-text contradiction because the agent or employee can release all of the confidentional information, creating no repurcussions. However, it is up for interpretation because Section 2, mentions that SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the SUPPLIER is responsible for ensuring its employees and agents comply with the agreement. The changed text removes this responsibility, directly contradicting Section 2, which states that the SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence. Without the SUPPLIER's explicit responsibility for its agents and employees, there is a contradiction regarding who is accountable for maintaining confidentiality, because the agent or employee can release all of the confidential information without any repercussions and the Supplier cannot be held responsible for it.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The construction, validity and performance hereof shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Connecticut, USA. The parties hereby agree to submit all disputes hereunder to arbitration in Connecticut, USA in accordance with the rules of the International Chamber of Commerce . All arbitration proceedings shall be conducted exclusively in the English language before a single arbitrator. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if SUPPLIER is alleged to have breached this Agreement, then GK shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The construction, validity and performance hereof shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Connecticut, USA. The parties hereby agree to submit all disputes hereunder to arbitration in Connecticut, USA in accordance with the rules of the International Chamber of Commerce . All arbitration proceedings shall be conducted exclusively in the English language before a single arbitrator.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence: "Notwithstanding the foregoing, if SUPPLIER is alleged to have breached this Agreement, then GK shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction." it restricts GK’s legal options in case of a breach by SUPPLIER, now their only option is arbitration. Previously, GK had the option of seeking injunctive relief from a court if SUPPLIER breached the agreement. The loss of this option contradicts the intention of protecting confidential information as stated throughout the agreement, because a court might be a faster option than arbitration, however, now it does not exists.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed GK to seek injunctive relief from any court if SUPPLIER breached the agreement. The changed text removes this option, limiting GK's recourse to only arbitration. The contradicted text states that SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence. Because injunctive relief might be a faster option to protect confidential information than arbitration, the removal of this option creates a contradiction because the changed text limits how the Confidential Information will be protected. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.
